athlete.name: Omar Lima
athlete.current_race_target: Build a runnable base — first trail 10K

_comment: EDIT THIS FILE TO LOG PROGRESS. For each metric, update 'value' and 'date'. Numbers have NO quotes (32); dates HAVE quotes ("2026-06-26"); keep the comma at the end of each line. To record history, add to the 'history' array: {"value": 212.3, "date": "2026-02-01"}. Status auto-computes: green = at/above pacer, yellow = within 10%, red = >10% below. Lab and protocol-only fields are tracked for trend, not scored.

metrics.name: Visceral Fat
metrics.note: DEXA visceral fat. Lower is better for metabolic health.

metrics.name: Walking Asymmetry
metrics.note: LIMP DETECTOR. Latest spike to 19% (6-mo avg is 10%). One high reading isn't a diagnosis - watch whether it stays elevated. Apple flags >3%.

metrics.name: Double Support Time
metrics.note: % of stride both feet down. Healthy 20-40%; you're at 33.4%, upper-normal. Watch for upward drift.

metrics.name: Walking Speed
metrics.note: iPhone walking pace (not running). 6-mo range 1-4.3 mph.

metrics.name: Walking Steadiness
metrics.note: Apple rating: OK (logged as 100). Stable all 6 months.

metrics.name: Walking Asymmetry (6mo avg)
metrics.note: 6-month average - the stable baseline. Smooths out single-day spikes.

metrics.name: Six-Minute Walk
metrics.note: Predicted walk distance / exercise capacity. 500m is Apple's estimate ceiling.

metrics.name: Ground Contact Time
metrics.note: Running: time each foot is on the ground. Lower = more efficient. Elite ~200ms.

metrics.name: Running Stride Length
metrics.note: Running stride. Will lengthen as run fitness builds.

metrics.name: Vertical Oscillation
metrics.note: Running: vertical bounce. Lower = less wasted energy. Good runners 6-9 cm.

metrics.name: Stair Speed Down
metrics.note: Descending stair speed - mobility/control marker. Watch for drops.

metrics.name: PACER Test
metrics.note: Stop after two misses. Retarget each retest.

metrics.name: Farmer Carry 70s
metrics.note: Fixed weight, max time (or log distance).

metrics.name: HR Recovery (1 min)
metrics.note: Drop 1 min after standardized cardio effort.

metrics.name: Sauna
metrics.note: ~4 sessions x 15 min. Cardiovascular benefit peaks 4-7x/wk (Finnish cohort).

metrics.name: Cold Plunge
metrics.note: Soberg Principle: 11 min/wk over 2-3 sessions, ~1-3 min each. More isn't better.

metrics.name: Steam Sauna
metrics.note: Heat exposure dosing similar to dry sauna; combine total heat time.

metrics.name: Red Light Therapy
metrics.note: 3-5 sessions/wk, 10-20 min each. Rest days between (biphasic dose response).

metrics.name: Wim Hof Breathing
metrics.note: Official method: ~20 min daily, 3-4 rounds. Always seated/lying, never near water.

metrics.name: ALT
metrics.note: Above range but improving (was 68 in Feb).

metrics.name: Thyroglobulin Ab
metrics.note: Above range. Matches known mildly elevated TgAb.

metrics.name: LDL Pattern
metrics.note: Pattern B (less favorable). Goal: shift to A.

metrics.name: B12 (MMA)
metrics.note: Methylmalonic acid — B12 functional marker, in range.

protocol.thyroid_history: Right thyroid nodule 7.7 cm; multinodular thyroid; tracheal deviation present; cancer ruled out on prior workup.

groups.title: Supplement Stack
groups.note: Selenium and NAD+ removed per latest review.

groups.title: Performance Goals
items: Strength: bodyweight bench, squat, deadlift, RDL
Body comp: cut fat, hold/build lean mass
Recovery: raise HRV, improve sleep continuity, fewer awakenings

current_focus.why: Foundation pillar. Better sleep raises HRV and recovery before the aerobic build.

episodes: Know Your Body — baseline assessments on camera

assessments: Longevity tests (sit-to-stand, balance, grip, HR recovery)
Function Health labs (Q3 — recheck lipids, ApoB, Vit D)
Max-strength test (est. 1RM: DL, bench, front squat)
Power test (vertical, broad jump, med ball)

episodes: Recovery stack — sauna, cold, red light

publishing.cadence: 4 long-form + 12 shorts / month · anchored to Friday (your strongest day)

weekly.desc: One lesson I'm applying this week — tied to Current Focus. Practical, single-topic (your best-performing format).
Edited 5-10 min, posted Friday AM to ride your biggest weekly spike. Exercise Library or Independence Through Movement. The editor's video.
30-60 sec clip from Friday's long-form. Drives weekend viewers to the full video.
What happened this week — catches the weekend-warrior audience your analytics flagged on Sundays. Follow the journey.

publishing.schedule_note: Based on 28-day analytics: spikes on Thu/Fri/Sun. Friday confirmed (data + general YouTube pattern). Sunday is a strong hypothesis — verify over the next month before locking. Mon/Tue remain quiet baseline days.
monthly_long: Week 4 — Project 50 Documentary (topic chosen from the month's data)

locations.intro: Seven sets, seven aspects of becoming a 50-year athlete. Each location signals a type of content so the series reads as a show, not a feed.
